interactions.summary: Sneako connects pride, Satan, racism, Israel, and left/right exhaustion; Jiang answers with Robin DiAngelo as an example of DEI as cult commerce built on white guilt and racial separation rather than empathy.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Sneako asks or frames the issue; Jiang answers in the main analytic voice where present.

speaker_notes.note: Machine diarization is consistent in this packet: SPEAKER_00 is Sneako/interviewer and SPEAKER_01 is Jiang, except where intro/outro material is host-only.

claims.claim: Jiang describes Robin DiAngelo’s White Fragility seminars as expensive rituals that tell white suburban women they are racist while excluding Black participants.
He argues that the supposed anti-racist seminar actually prevents interracial contact and empathy, turning protected Black people into objects of guilt.

signature_moments.moment: Jiang’s zoo image attacks anti-racism that protects people by forbidding contact with them.
signature_moments.source_phrase: Put black people in a zoo
signature_moments.why_it_matters: The shocking image carries his claim that DEI reenacts separation under moral language.

signature_moments.moment: Robin DiAngelo is called a cult leader rather than merely a flawed author.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It connects identity politics to religious manipulation and profit.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Robin DiAngelo’s framework as Jiang presents it: white people are born racist and must pay for anti-racist self-reflection seminars.
